records.ety: [OE. manuel, F. manuel, L. manualis, fr. manus hand; prob. akin to AS. mund hand, protection, OHG. munt, G. mündel a ward, vormund guardian, Icel. mund hand. Cf. Emancipate, Legerdemain, Maintain, Manage, Manner, Manure, Mound a hill.]

defs: Of or pertaining to the hand.
Performed by a person using physical as contrasted with mental effort; as, manual labor.
Done or made by the hand. In some contexts, contrasted with automatic or mechanical.

records.ety: [Cf. F. manuel, LL. manuale. See Manual, a.]

defs: A small book, such as may be carried in the hand, or conveniently handled; a handbook; specifically, the service book of the Roman Catholic Church.
quotes: This manual of laws, styled the Confessor's Laws.

defs: A keyboard of an organ or harmonium for the fingers, as distinguished from the pedals; a clavier, or set of keys.
A prescribed exercise in the systematic handing of a weapon; as, the manual of arms; the manual of the sword; the manual of the piece (cannon, mortar, etc.).
